title: pseudotsuga taxifolia var. glauca
intro: Pseudotsuga taxifolia var. glauca, also known as the White Fir, is a coniferous evergreen tree that belongs to the Pinaceae family. The bark of the White Fir is rich in salicin, a compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a valuable medicinal resource. The plant is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and promote relaxation. The herbal preparations of White Fir include tea infusions, essential oils, and tinctures, which can be used to relieve stress, improve sleep, and boost the immune system.
